creator: riggs@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Vladimir Omelchuk from comment #4)

Yes, rebuilding llvm36 does certainly not fix it because rust does not build with llvm36 anymore.

It seems the problem is that you have a local installation of rust 1.9.0 (or residuals of it) in /usr/local. The compiler complains that there is a file /usr/local/lib/librustc_llvm-d16b8f0e.so which should not be there.

Note that you cannot build rust while another rust package is already installed. Please remove the previous rust installation(s) completely before trying again.
